question: Create a `MyString` class that manages a dynamic character array. Implement both a copy constructor and an assignment operator (`operator=`). Explain when each of these is invoked through code examples.

test_cases.expected:
--- Demonstrating MyString Class ---
Constructor invoked for: Hello
s1: Hello
Copy Constructor invoked for: Hello (from Hello)
s2 (copy of s1): Hello

Calling demonstrateCopy(s1)...
Copy Constructor invoked for: Hello (from Hello)
Inside demonstrateCopy function. Value: Hello
Destructor invoked for: Hello
Back in main after demonstrateCopy(s1).
Constructor invoked for: World
s3: World

Assigning s1 to s3 (s3 = s1)...
Assignment Operator invoked for: Hello (from Hello)
s3 (after assignment from s1): Hello
Constructor invoked for: 
s4 (default): 

Assigning s3 to s4 (s4 = s3)...
Assignment Operator invoked for: Hello (from Hello)
s4 (after assignment from s3): Hello

Demonstrating self-assignment (s1 = s1)...
Assignment Operator invoked for: Hello (from Hello)
s1 (after self-assignment): Hello

Modifying s1 to see independence of copies/assignments.
s1 (modified): Hello C++
s2 (original copy): Hello
s3 (original assignment): Hello
s4 (original assignment): Hello

--- End of main ---
Destructor invoked for: Hello C++
Destructor invoked for: Hello
Destructor invoked for: Hello
Destructor invoked for: Hello
